scenario: Auditors ask for all DMN evaluations done in the past year -- what decisions were evaluated, with what inputs / outputs, when, for which process instance.
question: Where does Camunda 8 store DMN evaluation history?

options.text: Only Optimize stores DMN evaluation history for auditing purposes -- this misidentifies the primary tool for per-evaluation decision audit records. Optimize provides aggregate analytics and trend analysis over decision evaluations but is not the per-instance granular record store. Operate's Decision Instances view stores per-evaluation records including inputs, outputs, matched rules, hit policy, timestamps, and parent process instance links. For "all evaluations in the past year" audit requests, Operate is the primary source. Optimize complements with aggregate views and rule-frequency analytics.

Teams must implement custom logging for every DMN evaluation to maintain an audit trail -- this ignores the built-in decision instance tracking. Operate automatically captures per-evaluation decision instance records without any custom instrumentation. These records include all inputs, outputs, matched rules, and metadata. Custom logging is only needed for additional business context beyond what the standard decision instance record captures, not as a substitute for the built-in audit mechanism.

Operate's Decisions / Decision Instances views capture each DMN evaluation with full detail: the decision ID, version, evaluation inputs, outputs produced, matched rules, hit policy applied, evaluation timestamp, and a link to the parent process instance when evaluated from a Business Rule Task. md:29, Operate exposes READ_DECISION_DEFINITION and READ_DECISION_INSTANCE authorisations. These records are searchable by decision ID, time range, and input values. For long-term audit retention, configure the retention policy accordingly.

Operate captures every DMN evaluation as a decision instance record, including the decision ID, evaluated inputs and outputs, matched rules for decision tables, the hit policy applied, evaluation timestamp, and the parent process instance key for evaluations called from Business Rule Tasks. These records are searchable and filterable in the Operate UI and queryable via the Operate API.

a.text: Incorrect. Operate's Decision Instances feature captures per-evaluation records: Searchable: by decision ID, time range, parent process instance, input values. Detailed view: inputs, outputs, matched rules, hit policy semantics. Linked to parent process: when evaluated from BPMN Business Rule Task, the decision instance links to the process instance. For audit / compliance: Retention: configure Operate's retention to cover the compliance window (e.g., 7 years). Export: programmatic export via Operate API to long-term store for archival. Filtering: ops can search for specific decisions / instances / time periods. Operate is for per-instance and per-evaluation audit -- granular records. Optimize complements: Aggregate analytics: how many evaluations per day; trends; rule hit frequencies. Insights: rules that never fire (possible removal candidates); rules that always fire (possible default). For "all evaluations in the past year for audit," Operate provides the records; Optimize provides aggregate views.

b.text: Incorrect. Both useful; Operate has per-instance.
